acds_handlers.h(3) Library Functions Manual acds_handlers.h(3)

NAME

acds_handlers.h - ACIP Discovery Server (ACDS) packet handlers.

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct acip_acds_callbacks_t
ACDS packet handler callbacks.

Functions


asciichat_error_t acip_handle_acds_packet (acip_transport_t *transport, packet_type_t type, const void *payload, size_t payload_len, const char *client_ip, const acip_acds_callbacks_t *callbacks)
Handle incoming ACDS packet with O(1) dispatch.

Detailed Description

ACIP Discovery Server (ACDS) packet handlers.

Provides O(1) array-based packet dispatching for ascii-chat Discovery Server. Handles session management, WebRTC signaling, and discovery protocol packets.

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

January 2026

Definition in file acds_handlers.h.

Function Documentation

asciichat_error_t acip_handle_acds_packet (acip_transport_t * transport, packet_type_t type, const void * payload, size_t payload_len, const char * client_ip, const acip_acds_callbacks_t * callbacks)

Handle incoming ACDS packet with O(1) dispatch. Dispatches packet to appropriate callback based on type. Uses array-based lookup for constant-time handler selection.

Parameters

transport Transport instance for sending responses and accessing per-client state
type Packet type
payload Packet payload
payload_len Payload length
client_ip Client IP address string
callbacks Application callbacks

Returns

ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure

Definition at line 116 of file acds_handlers.c.

118                                                                                   {
119   if (!callbacks) {
120     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid callbacks");
121   }
122 
123   // O(1) dispatch via hash table lookup
124   int idx = acds_handler_hash_lookup(g_acds_handler_hash, type);
125   if (idx < 0) {
126     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Unhandled ACDS packet type: %d from %s", type, client_ip);
127   }
128 
129   return g_acds_handlers[idx](payload, payload_len, transport, client_ip, callbacks);
130 }




References ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, and SET_ERRNO.


Referenced by acds_client_handler(), and
    acds_websocket_client_handler().
